description: Dash walks the ocean floor as bioluminescent creatures light up the dark. A 40-second explainer on how living light works in the deep sea — and why the ocean glows.
thumbnail_concept: Dash mid-stride on dark blue seafloor, oversized eyes reflecting cyan glow; behind him a trail of glowing plankton and a soft jelly silhouette; bold title text top-third: 'THE OCEAN IS GLOWING'; high contrast, 9:16.
voice_direction: Energetic, curious, slightly hushed wonder under water metaphor — fast but clear; smile on discovery lines; never robotic.
music_direction: Soft aquatic pulse, low synth pads, gentle sparkle motifs on glow reveals; duck under VO; swell on final wide glow shot.
scientific_notes: Bioluminescence = light produced by living organisms via chemical reactions (often luciferin + luciferase).
Common in marine environments; functions include predation, defense, communication.
Keep claims general; avoid overstating specific species behavior without naming them carefully.

script.hook: The ocean is secretly glowing — and most of that light is alive.
body: Down here, sunlight barely reaches… so life makes its own.
Tiny plankton flash like blue sparks when the water moves.
Jellies and deep-sea fish use chemical light — a reaction that turns energy into a glow.
Some use it to hunt. Some use it to escape. Some use it to talk.
script.payoff: So next time you hear the ocean is dark… remember: it's also a night sky underwater.
script.full_text: The ocean is secretly glowing — and most of that light is alive. Down here, sunlight barely reaches… so life makes its own. Tiny plankton flash like blue sparks when the water moves. Jellies and deep-sea fish use chemical light — a reaction that turns energy into a glow. Some use it to hunt. Some use it to escape. Some use it to talk. It's called bioluminescence — living light. So next time you hear the ocean is dark… remember: it's also a night sky underwater.

scenes.narration: The ocean is secretly glowing — and most of that light is alive.
